title: dryopteris iranica
intro: Dryopteris iranica, also known as Iranian wood fern, is a species of fern native to Iran and other parts of the Middle East. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and aiding in digestion, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves can be dried and used in herbal infusions or added to soups and stews for its medicinal value.
